lead: This week's tech brief highlights innovative startups, significant VC deals, and the latest advancements in AI, alongside notable developments in the Australian tech ecosystem.

stories.title: Meet the Melbourne-built robot now in dozens of aged care homes
stories.summary: A new humanoid robot named Abi, created by a 26-year-old Melbourne inventor, is now assisting in aged care homes. Designed to communicate in 90 languages and engage with residents through storytelling and bubble-blowing, Abi aims to enhance the emotional well-being of the elderly, showcasing a novel application of robotics in healthcare.

stories.title: Amazon’s new podcast strategy: Monetize everything
stories.summary: Amazon is revamping its podcasting strategy to focus on monetization, reflecting a significant shift in how the company approaches audio content. This move could reshape the podcasting landscape, as Amazon seeks to leverage its vast resources to create a more profitable model for creators and advertisers alike.

stories.title: US State Dept orders global warning about alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
stories.summary: The US State Department has issued a global warning regarding alleged AI technology thefts by Chinese firms, including DeepSeek. This announcement highlights ongoing concerns about intellectual property security in the AI sector and the geopolitical implications of emerging technologies.

stories.title: Forced Windows updates can now be paused forever
stories.summary: Microsoft has introduced a feature allowing Windows 11 users to indefinitely pause updates, addressing user frustrations with forced updates. This change enhances user control over their devices, allowing them to manage updates according to their schedules, which is a significant improvement in user experience.

stories.title: An OpenAI-linked news outlet appears to be entirely AI-generated
stories.summary: A new news outlet, reportedly funded by an OpenAI super PAC, has been revealed to be entirely AI-generated. This development raises questions about the future of journalism and the role of AI in content creation, as it blurs the lines between human and machine-generated news.

stories.title: I ran the 20-minute Apple Watch calibration test - and my data got more accurate
stories.summary: A recent article details the benefits of calibrating the Apple Watch for improved accuracy in health data tracking. This simple process can enhance the device's performance during workouts, showcasing how users can leverage technology for better personal health insights.

stories.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
stories.summary: In a unique twist on real estate transactions, a property in the Bay Area is being offered for sale with the stipulation that buyers must possess equity in Anthropic, an AI startup. This reflects the increasing intersection of tech investments and traditional real estate markets.

stories.title: Google’s new gradient icon design is coming to more apps
stories.summary: Google is expanding its new gradient icon design across more of its applications, moving away from the traditional uniform circle design. This aesthetic shift not only modernizes the interface but also aligns with the integration of AI features across Google's services.

stories.title: Creative Assembly drops first look at the Alien: Isolation sequel
stories.summary: Creative Assembly has unveiled the first look at the sequel to the popular game Alien: Isolation. This announcement has generated excitement among fans and highlights the ongoing innovation within the gaming industry, particularly in narrative-driven experiences.

stories.title: Canadian premier wants to ban social media and AI chatbots for kids in Manitoba
stories.summary: The Premier of Manitoba has proposed a ban on social media and AI chatbots for children, aiming to protect youth from potential harms associated with these technologies. This initiative reflects a growing concern over the impact of digital engagement on younger generations.
